shortDescription: AI-native lightweight document storage — save, retrieve, search, and delete working documents backed by Azure Blob + Cosmos DB
orchestratorUseCases: save research notes, drafts, and working documents during long-running tasks
retrieve previously saved documents by ID or keyword search
list all stored documents for the user
delete documents that are no longer needed

tools.name: docs_save
tools.description: Saves a new document or updates an existing document in the user's personal document store. The document content should be Markdown. On create, returns a new document ID. To update, pass the existing docId — the content and title will be overwritten and updatedAt refreshed. Use this during research tasks to checkpoint work, save drafts, or revise saved documents.

docId.description: Optional UUID of an existing document to update. If omitted, a new document is created.

title.description: A short descriptive title for the document (max 255 chars)

content.description: The document body in Markdown format

tags.description: Optional list of tags for categorization (e.g. ['research', 'draft', 'project-x']). On update, omitting tags preserves existing tags.

tools.name: docs_get
tools.description: Retrieves the full content of a document by its ID. Returns the title, tags, and Markdown body.

docId.description: The UUID of the document to retrieve

tools.name: docs_list
tools.description: Lists document metadata (id, title, tags, size, dates) for all documents stored by the user. Optionally filter by tag. Does not return document content — use docs_get to retrieve content.

tag.description: Optional tag to filter by (exact match)

tools.name: docs_search
tools.description: Searches documents by keyword — matches against title and the first 500 characters of content. Returns matching document metadata. Use docs_get to read the full content of a result.

query.description: Keyword or phrase to search for in document titles and content summaries

tools.name: docs_delete
tools.description: Permanently deletes a document and its content from blob storage. This cannot be undone. Only use when the user explicitly asks to delete a document.

docId.description: The UUID of the document to delete
